rulings.Case Title: JANET WHITAKER et al VS. BORG-WARNER CORPORATION BY ITS SUCCESSOR IN et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, FEBRUARY 14, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3, Defendant Volkswagen Group of America, Inc.¿s motion to strike portions of plaintiffs¿ complaint relating to punitive damages and loss of consortium is off calendar as moot given the ruling on defendant¿s demurrer. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JANET WHITAKER et al VS. BORG-WARNER CORPORATION BY ITS SUCCESSOR IN et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, FEBRUARY 14, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3, Defendant Volkswagen Group of America, Inc.¿s demurrer to each cause of action in plaintiffs¿ complaint is sustained with ten days leave to amend to give plaintiffs an opportunity, if they are able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71. Defendant¿s special demurrer to the fourth cause of action (loss of consortium) is overruled. Plaintiffs¿ complaint sufficiently pleads a cause of action for loss of consortium and the authorities cited by defendant do not demonstrate that a cause of action for loss of consortium is precluded in a wrongful death action. Defendant¿s demurrer was timely filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
